steps.title: Check current cloudevents setup
steps.description:
Verify your cloudevents version:
CloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)
This feature requires cloudevents as a dependency.

steps.title: Review cloudevents configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant cloudevents configuration:
Event streams often have an ordering which is important for a set of events for the same entity, but not for events of different entities.

steps.title: Use CloudEvents extension attributes for event ordering
steps.description:
CloudEvents v1.0 addresses event ordering through extension attributes rather than a core 'ordinal' field. Use the `sequence` extension attribute to indicate event order, and the Partitioning extension for key-based routing:
```json
{
  "specversion": "1.0",
  "type": "com.example.user.created",
  "source": "/users",
  "id": "A234-1234-1234",
  "sequence": "1",
  "sequencetype": "Integer",
  "partitionkey": "user-alice",
  "data": { "name": "alice" }
}
```
The `sequence` extension provides explicit ordering per source, while `partitionkey` enables consumers and intermediaries to route related events to the same processing partition. See the CloudEvents Extensions catalog for details.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Event keys" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: The event ordering/key concept was addressed as part of the CloudEvents v1.0 specification work. Rather than adding an explicit 'ordinal' field, the spec recommends using extension attributes (like 'sequence') for ordering when needed. The Partitioning extension provides guidance for event routing based on keys.

prerequisites.description: CloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)
